channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: CodeQL 2.25.2 adds Kotlin 2.3.20 support and other updates
item.description: CodeQL 2.25.2 has been released, adding support for Kotlin 2.3.20 and other updates to enhance GitHub's static analysis capabilities for security issues. This update is significant as it improves developers' ability to identify vulnerabilities in their code.

item.title: I tried Google's new desktop app for Windows, and I'll never search the old way again
item.description: Google's new desktop app for Windows offers a faster way to access tools like Gemini, Lens, and Search, promising a more efficient user experience. This release could change how users interact with Google's services on their desktops.

item.title: Anthropic's Project Glasswing CVE tally is still anyone's guess
item.description: Anthropic's Project Glasswing allows select companies to test its new model, Mythos, for finding vulnerabilities, but the actual number of discovered issues remains unclear. This initiative raises questions about the model's safety and effectiveness.

item.title: Don't let the bot play doctor! AI gets early diagnoses wrong 80% of the time
item.description: Research indicates that AI models, including LLMs, are inaccurate in early diagnostic reasoning, getting it wrong 80% of the time. This raises concerns about the reliability of AI in medical contexts.

item.title: Customers revolt as GitHub Copilot 'fixes' rate limits
item.description: GitHub Copilot users are facing issues due to a bug that miscalculated token usage, leading to rapid exhaustion of their subscription allowances. This has sparked customer dissatisfaction and concerns about the service's reliability.

item.title: Shoe company says it's getting into AI infrastructure and yes this is the top
item.description: A shoe company is venturing into AI infrastructure, drawing parallels to past branding shifts in unrelated industries. This trend reflects a growing interest in AI across diverse sectors.

item.title: OpenAI updates its Agents SDK to help enterprises build safer, more capable agents
item.description: OpenAI has updated its Agents SDK to enhance the development of safer and more capable AI agents for enterprises. This update reflects the increasing demand for robust AI solutions in business environments.

item.title: Hightouch reaches $100M ARR fueled by marketing tools powered by AI
item.description: Hightouch has achieved $100 million in annual recurring revenue, driven by its AI-powered marketing tools, showcasing rapid growth in the AI marketing sector. This highlights the increasing reliance on AI for business efficiency.

item.title: LinkedIn data shows AI isn’t to blame for hiring decline… yet
item.description: LinkedIn reports a 20% decline in hiring since 2022, attributing it to higher interest rates rather than AI. This insight suggests that economic factors are currently more influential than technological ones in job markets.

item.title: AI learning app Gizmo levels up with 13M users and a $22M investment
item.description: Gizmo, an AI learning app, has reached 13 million users and secured $22 million in Series A funding, indicating strong market interest in AI-driven educational tools. This growth reflects the increasing demand for innovative learning solutions.

item.title: Can AI judge journalism? A Thiel-backed startup says yes, even if it risks chilling whistleblowers
item.description: Objection, a startup backed by Peter Thiel, plans to use AI to evaluate journalism, allowing users to challenge stories. Critics argue this could deter whistleblowers and alter media accountability.

item.title: Enable Copilot cloud agent via custom properties
item.description: GitHub now allows enterprise admins to enable the Copilot cloud agent on a per-organization basis, enhancing control over its deployment.

item.title: Google rolls out a native Gemini app for Mac
item.description: Google has launched a native Gemini app for Mac, enabling users to share their screen and receive real-time assistance from the AI.

item.title: Trump’s posting even more AI-generated Trump-Jesus fan art
item.description: Donald Trump is increasingly sharing AI-generated fan art depicting himself as Jesus, reflecting the intersection of politics and AI-generated content.

item.title: 'Like handing out the blueprint to a bank vault': Why AI led one company to abandon open source
item.description: Cal has shifted its flagship open-source program to a proprietary model due to concerns over AI exploiting its open code.

item.title: iPhone charging slowly? 6 quick fixes to try before blaming your battery
item.description: If your iPhone is charging slowly, there are several troubleshooting tips to try before assuming a battery issue, including checking your setup.

item.title: Roku TV vs. Fire Stick: Why I'm looking beyond streaming resolution when comparing the two
item.description: Roku and Amazon have released new versions of their streaming sticks, prompting a comparison beyond just streaming resolution.

item.title: After sale of its shoe business, Allbirds pivots to AI
item.description: After selling its shoe business, Allbirds is pivoting to AI, rebranding as NewBird AI and securing $50 million in financing.

item.title: Google launches a Gemini AI app on Mac
item.description: Google's Gemini AI app for Mac allows users to interact with the assistant seamlessly while working on other tasks, enhancing productivity.

item.title: French cops free mother and son after 20-hour crypto kidnap ordeal
item.description: French police rescued a mother and her son after a 20-hour kidnapping related to a crypto extortion case, highlighting ongoing security concerns.

item.title: Reid Hoffman weighs in on the ‘tokenmaxxing’ debate
item.description: Reid Hoffman discusses the 'tokenmaxxing' debate, suggesting that while tracking AI token usage can indicate adoption, it should not be seen as a direct measure of productivity without context.

item.title: Adobe’s new Firefly AI assistant can use Creative Cloud apps to complete tasks
item.description: Adobe has launched Firefly, an AI assistant that integrates with its Creative Cloud apps to automate tasks, enhancing user productivity across various platforms.

item.title: Why your TV wowed you in the store but looks unnatural at home - and how to fix it ASAP
item.description: Many TVs appear overly bright and unnatural at home due to store settings; adjusting these settings can improve viewing experience significantly.

item.title: AI is getting better at your job, but you have time to adjust, according to MIT
item.description: MIT research suggests that AI could be 'minimally sufficient' for certain job tasks by 2029, urging workers to prepare for this shift.

item.title: langchain-mixpeek added to PyPI
item.description: LangChain has integrated Mixpeek into PyPI, enhancing AI agents with a multimodal retriever tool for improved functionality.

item.title: pollinations-claude-code 0.2.1
item.description: The Claude Code CLI plugin for Pollinations.ai allows users to generate various media types, utilizing over 50 models for creative tasks.

item.title: The $100B Marketer Trust Challenge: Advertising in Conversational AI
item.description: As advertising in conversational AI platforms grows, marketers are pushing for new standards of trust and verification to ensure ad effectiveness.

item.title: VCs are flooding Anthropic with offers to invest at up to an $800 billion valuation
item.description: Anthropic is attracting significant investment offers, with valuations reaching up to $800 billion, indicating strong confidence in its future potential.

item.title: sage-ai-cli 1.13.12
item.description: The latest update of Sage introduces a local-first AI coding CLI, similar to Claude Code, utilizing free and open models for coding tasks.

item.title: UBS Analysts Say AI Models May Replace Some Software Incumbents
item.description: UBS analysts warn that new AI models from companies like Anthropic and OpenAI could disrupt traditional enterprise software markets.

item.title: Vibhor Kumar: AI-Ready PostgreSQL 18 Is Out: Why AI Applications Win or Lose at the Seams
item.description: Vibhor Kumar discusses the challenges AI projects face, emphasizing that failures often stem from issues surrounding data governance and production realities rather than weak models. This highlights the importance of robust infrastructure for successful AI deployment.

item.title: JUST NOW: JD Vance announces FBI Director Kash Patel is SURGING resources to prosecute the leftists FEDERALLY who assaulted TPUSA journalist Savanah Hernandez in Minnesota
item.description: JD Vance announced that FBI Director Kash Patel is allocating more resources to prosecute individuals involved in the assault of a journalist in Minnesota. This move signals a federal response to political violence.

item.title: mimir-observe 0.4.2
item.description: The release of mimir-observe 0.4.2 introduces auto-instrumentation and visibility features for AI agents, enhancing the OpenAI Agents SDK and Claude SDK. This update aims to improve the monitoring and management of AI systems.

item.title: torch-tk added to PyPI
item.description: The addition of torch-tk to PyPI simplifies the training, checkpoint management, and diagnostics of PyTorch models. This tool aims to enhance the user experience for developers working with PyTorch.

item.title: Apple App Store threatened to remove Grok over deepfakes: letter
item.description: Apple threatened to remove Grok from the App Store over its involvement in generating sexualized deepfakes, as detailed in a letter to senators. This raises concerns about the regulation of deepfake technology.

item.title: OpenAI Releases Cyber Model to Limited Group in Race With Mythos - Bloomberg.com
item.description: OpenAI has released a new cyber model, GPT-5.4-Cyber, to a limited group, following a competitive announcement from Anthropic's Mythos. This development indicates a race in advancing AI capabilities for cybersecurity.

item.title: beatscore added to PyPI
item.description: The beatscore tool has been added to PyPI, allowing users to convert text into music, showcasing the creative applications of AI. It was developed during a hackathon using ElevenLabs technology.

item.title: perplexityai 0.32.0
item.description: Perplexityai 0.32.0 is the official Python library for the perplexity API, enhancing accessibility for developers. This update aims to streamline integration with AI applications.

item.title: stele-context 1.1.1
item.description: Stele-context 1.1.1 introduces persistent memory for AI coding agents, allowing them to retain information across conversations. This update aims to improve the efficiency of coding assistants.

item.title: Secure AI agent access patterns to AWS resources using Model Context Protocol
item.description: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) allows AI agents to access AWS resources dynamically, adapting their interactions based on context. This represents a shift from traditional deterministic coding approaches.

item.title: llama-index-workflows 2.19.0
item.description: LlamaIndex Workflows 2.19.0 introduces an event-driven, async-first approach to manage AI application execution flows, enhancing control over Agents. This update is significant for developers looking to streamline AI processes.

item.title: llama-agents-client 0.3.5
item.description: Llama Agents Client 0.3.5 is an HTTP client designed for connecting to LlamaIndex workflow servers, facilitating interaction with AI workflows. This tool is crucial for developers needing efficient communication with their AI applications.

item.title: llama-agents-server 0.4.5
item.description: Llama Agents Server 0.4.5 serves as an HTTP server for deploying LlamaIndex workflows as web services, allowing for easier accessibility and integration. This is important for developers looking to deploy AI solutions seamlessly.

item.title: rqmd 0.2.6
item.description: RQMD 0.2.6 is a CLI tool for managing Requirements Markdown, featuring GitHub Copilot AI integration through a VS Code extension. This tool enhances productivity for developers managing project requirements.

item.title: language-model-common 2.0.17
item.description: Language Model Common 2.0.17 provides a framework to enhance LangChain and manage loading configurations, improving the functionality of language models. This update is significant for developers working with complex AI systems.

item.title: langchain-openterms added to PyPI
item.description: LangChain has integrated the OpenTerms protocol, enabling permission-aware AI agents. This integration is crucial for developers focusing on ethical AI usage and compliance.

item.title: aidebate 0.4.0
item.description: Aidebate 0.4.0 is a multi-agent AI debate orchestrator that runs structured debates with various AI agents and features a web UI. This tool is significant for researchers and developers interested in AI interactions.

item.title: copilot-image-gen-mcp added to PyPI
item.description: Copilot Image Gen MCP has been added to PyPI, enabling AI image generation through Microsoft Copilot. This addition is important for developers looking to integrate image generation capabilities into their applications.

item.title: vision-agents-plugins-gemini 0.5.3
item.description: Vision Agents Plugins Gemini 0.5.3 integrates Google Gemini LLM with Vision Agents, enhancing AI capabilities in visual tasks. This integration is significant for developers working on AI-driven visual applications.

item.title: vision-agents-plugins-xai 0.5.3
item.description: Vision Agents Plugins XAI 0.5.3 introduces explainable AI features for stream agents, promoting transparency in AI decision-making. This is crucial for developers focused on ethical AI practices.
